ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for chili crab meat sauce with fried mantou specifically; mapped to the closest parent class 'crab dish' (FOODON_03311737). The composite nature of this dish (seafood sauce plus fried bread) is not captured by a single FoodOn term.
ontology.foodex2_description: Crustaceans, cooked — used as the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for the crab meat sauce component of this composite dish. The fried mantou component is not separately coded; noted as a composite dish limitation.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for chili crab meat sauce with fried mantou. A0CKW (Crustaceans, cooked) is used as the primary anchor for the dominant seafood component. Composite dish coding is not fully supported in FoodEx2 for this preparation.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept found for chili crab meat sauce with fried mantou. SNOMED CT food coding for composite Singaporean dishes is not available as of 2024.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of chili crab meat sauce with 8 mini deep-fried mantou buns weighs approximately 600 g, comprising the crab meat sauce portion and the fried buns for dipping.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entry exists for chili crab meat sauce with mini fried mantou as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by component-based calculation using HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entries for cooked crab meat, chili crab sauce, and deep-fried mantou buns, weighted by approximate recipe proportions (crab meat sauce ~60%, fried mantou ~40% by weight). Input values from the GrabFood listing (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against HPB component data and retained where consistent. Energy 200 kcal/100g, protein 10g, fat 12.5g, carbohydrate 12.5g are plausible for this composite. Sodium 416.7mg/100g is consistent with HPB data for chili crab sauce preparations. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; University of Sydney GI Database — white bread and refined wheat bun analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chili crab meat sauce with fried mantou as a composite dish in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate contributor being deep-fried mantou buns (refined wheat flour, GI ~70 per University of Sydney GI Database for white bread and steamed buns) attenuated by the lower-GI crab meat sauce component (protein and fat slow glycaemic response). Weighted composite GI estimated at ~65 reflecting approximately 40% fried mantou by weight. Glycemic load per serving: GL = (65 × 75g carbohydrate in 600g serving) / 100 = 48.75. Carbohydrate per serving: 12.5g/100g × 600g = 75g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 416.7 mg per 100g; a full 600g serving delivers approximately 2,500 mg sodium, exceeding the WHO daily limit of 2,000 mg in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 150–200g of the sauce component, request reduced soy sauce and chili paste, and substitute steamed mantou for fried to reduce both sodium and caloric load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 12.5g/100g with a composite GI of approximately 65 (medium); a full 600g serving provides ~75g carbohydrate and a glycemic load of ~49, which is high for a single meal.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit the number of fried mantou buns consumed (each bun contributes high-GI refined starch), request steamed mantou to reduce GI and fat, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to moderate the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-fried mantou buns contribute trans fat (0.08g/100g) and elevated total fat (12.5g/100g); cholesterol is 50mg/100g; sodium at 416.7mg/100g further elevates cardiovascular risk; a full serving delivers approximately 2,500mg sodium.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid or strictly limit this dish due to the combination of high sodium, deep-fried fat content, trans fat from frying, and high glycemic load; if consumed, portion should not exceed 150g of sauce with steamed rather than fried mantou.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying of mantou buns generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) which promote systemic inflammation and worsen joint symptoms in rheumatoid arthritis.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request steamed mantou buns to eliminate AGE formation; the crab meat component provides beneficial omega-3 fatty acids and the ginger and garlic in the sauce offer anti-inflammatory polyphenols, making the sauce itself more tolerable if AGE exposure is minimised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chili Crab Meat Sauce with Mini Fried Buns (辣椒螃蟹肉酱配炸馒头) is a Singaporean Chinese seafood dish consisting of de-shelled crab meat simmered in a rich, spicy, and savoury chili-tomato sauce, served with eight pieces of deep-fried mini mantou buns for dipping. The sauce is prepared in a wok and thickened with beaten egg and cornstarch, producing a glossy, semi-thick gravy with a complex flavour profile combining heat from chili paste, umami from soy sauce, sweetness from tomato, and richness from egg. The crab meat contributes a delicate, slightly sweet seafood flavour. The deep-fried mantou buns are golden-brown, crispy on the outside and soft inside, designed to absorb the sauce. Per 100g, the dish provides approximately 200 kcal, 10g protein, 12.5g fat, 12.5g carbohydrate, 3.33g sugar, 0.83g dietary fibre, and 416.7mg sodium. Clinically, the dish is high in sodium (416.7mg/100g), making it unsuitable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease patients at full serving sizes. The deep-fried mantou buns contribute refined high-GI carbohydrates (composite GI ~65, medium) and trans fat from frying, raising concerns for Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease management. The crab meat provides beneficial protein and some omega-3 fatty acids. This dish is a celebrated icon of Singaporean cuisine, commonly served at seafood restaurants and hawker centres, and is associated with festive and celebratory dining occasions. It is not halal-certified in most preparations due to the use of crab (a crustacean) and potential cross-contamination. Allergens include crustaceans, egg, gluten (wheat in mantou), and soy.
common_user_queries: How many calories in chili crab with fried mantou?
Is chili crab suitable for diabetics?
Can I eat chili crab if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database component data for cooked crab meat, chili crab sauce, and deep-fried mantou buns. No single approved-source entry exists for this exact composite dish. Input values (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against HPB component data and are plausible. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for refined wheat bread and buns. Confidence is medium due to composite estimation and absence of a direct approved-source match for this specific dish.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-based estimates derived from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entries for cooked crab meat, chili crab sauce, and deep-fried mantou buns. No single HPB Foodo or USDA FoodData Central entry exists for this exact composite dish. Input values from GrabFood listing (ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were retained where consistent with HPB component data. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient breakdown, and moisture data for this composite Singaporean dish are not available in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024 for this specific preparation. 3. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for chili crab meat sauce with fried mantou exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Estimate is based on the dominant carbohydrate contributor (deep-fried refined wheat mantou, GI ~70) attenuated by the protein- and fat-rich crab sauce component. 4. Halal status is not confirmed; crab is a crustacean and is not universally accepted as halal in all Islamic jurisprudence schools. is_halal is set to false as a conservative default. 5. The authoritative HPB source URL used is https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/ (the official HPB Foodo food composition portal). The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was a third-party platform and has been replaced with the official HPB domain. 6. trans_fat_g of 0.08g/100g is retained from input; this is plausible for deep-fried mantou and reflects a small but non-negligible trans fat contribution from the frying process. 7. Ontology match types corrected to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2 per allowed values: ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'].
